name: shadcn/ui ecosystem libraries
description: Derived libraries that pair well with shadcn/ui (many with free tiers): Radix, CVA, tailwind-merge, Lucide, RHF+Zod, Sonner, Framer Motion, and block registries.

metadata.intent: Choose shadcn-compatible libraries for real products without breaking ownership or accessibility

must_use: Libraries that preserve accessibility and code ownership
must_not: Heavy UI libraries that replace owned components with opaque runtime dependencies
conventions: Prefer small, composable helpers that fit Tailwind + shadcn patterns.

steps.description: Radix UI: unstyled accessible primitives (keyboard, focus, ARIA). Use directly when you need behavior beyond what shadcn exposes.
class-variance-authority (CVA): scalable variants (size/intent/state) without conditional class soup. Non-negotiable for reusable primitives.
tailwind-merge + `cn()`: resolves conflicting classes and keeps overrides clean. Prefer this over manual class concatenation.
Lucide React: tree-shakable neutral icons. Use consistent icon sizing rules to maintain visual rhythm.
React Hook Form + Zod: performant forms with type-safe validation. Use Zod schemas for both UI validation and server boundaries when possible.
Sonner: lightweight accessible toasts that fit Tailwind UIs. Use for user feedback (success/error) without heavy UI kits.
Framer Motion: use selectively for high-impact transitions (dialog/page motion). Avoid turning every hover into JS animation; prefer CSS for simple interactions.
Blocks/registries (often with free tiers): use them as inspiration + copy-source patterns. Keep the shadcn ethos: copy code, own it, adapt it, and keep a11y intact.
